booking.title: Book a service
booking.subtitle: Tell us what you need and where your boat is. A technician will call you back within one business day.
community.intro_p1: Triton Marine operates from the harbor of Skala on the island of Patmos — one of the Dodecanese islands in the eastern Aegean, known for its centuries-old seafaring tradition and its UNESCO-protected monastic heritage.
community.intro_p2: Our yard sits minutes from open water, with direct access to protected waters and the main ferry port. For visiting yachts it's a natural stop; for local boat owners it's home base year-round.
community.intro_p3: Supporting the Patmian maritime community has been our purpose since day one: keeping the island's boats safe, serviceable, and sailing.
community.harbor_p1: Skala's sheltered bay has served merchants and sailors for over two thousand years. Today it hosts a modest commercial port, a daily ferry line, and a seasonal marina.
community.harbor_p2: The calm anchorage and year-round accessibility make Patmos one of the most reliable places in the eastern Aegean for boat maintenance and storage.
community.scholarship.headline: Learn the craft. Keep the harbor sailing.
community.scholarship.lead_p1: Every year Triton Marine opens a small number of paid scholarships to people who want to learn boatbuilding and marine repair from the ground up — alongside the shipwrights, mechanics, and electricians who keep the Patmian fleet alive.
community.scholarship.lead_p2: No prior experience needed. What we look for is patience, hands, and a willingness to spend a year on the water.
community.scholarship.why_lead: A working harbor needs working hands. Every year fewer young people grow up around boats, and the knowledge that built this fleet is harder to pass on. The scholarship is how we hold that line.
community.scholarship.why_card1_body: Traditional Aegean boatbuilding skills, kept alive in a working yard.
community.scholarship.why_card2_body: Senior shipwrights teach the way they were taught — at the bench, on the hull.
community.scholarship.why_card3_body: Apprentices leave with a trade, not just a certificate.
community.scholarship.why_card4_body: Local boats stay seaworthy because there is someone left who knows how.
community.scholarship.year_subtitle: Twelve months, four phases, one boat at the end of it.
community.scholarship.phase1_body: Yard safety, the workshop, materials, and the people you'll learn from.
community.scholarship.phase2_body: Plane, chisel, saw. Reading wood. The fundamentals every shipwright needs.
community.scholarship.phase3_body: On the cradle: caulking, fastening, ribs, planks. How a hull holds together.
community.scholarship.phase4_body: Splash, rig, sail. Take the boat out, find what you missed, fix it, and finish.
community.scholarship.apply_body: Applications are open to anyone who can commit to a full year in Patmos. We read every email and reply within five working days.
community.scholarship.eligibility_2: You can work legally in Greece or the EU.
community.scholarship.eligibility_3: You're comfortable with manual, full-day work.
community.scholarship.eligibility_4: You speak basic English or Greek.
community.scholarship.eligibility_5: You can live on Patmos year-round.
community.scholarship.deadline: Applications for the 2026 intake close 30 September 2025.
about.hero_title: A boatyard built on the harbor of Patmos
about.p1: Triton Marine is a family-run boatyard in Skala, on the Greek island of Patmos. We haul, store, repair, and build boats — for the local fleet, for visiting yachts, and for owners who've trusted us with their vessels for years.
about.p2: Our team is small and senior: shipwrights, engine technicians, and marine electricians who grew up around the harbor. We know the waters, the weather, and the quirks of every type of boat that calls the Dodecanese home.
about.p3: We operate year-round. Summer is launches, emergency repairs, and small-job turnarounds; winter is haul-outs, full refits, and custom builds. Whatever season you find us in, the goal is the same: get your boat back in the water, safe and running right.
faq.heading: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.intro: Anything else you'd like to know? Send us a message — we read every enquiry and reply within one business day.
related.heading: Related Services
contact.info_subtitle: The easiest way to reach us is the form below — or use any of the channels below.
contact.map_aria: Map of Triton Marine, Skala harbor, Patmos
about.intro_lead: A small, senior crew running a working boatyard on the harbor of Patmos — the same hands that built half the local fleet.
about.crew_lead: Twelve people. Three trades. One yard.
about.crew_role1_body: Wood, fiberglass, composite. The builders, the fixers, the people who shape a hull.
about.crew_role2_body: Inboard, outboard, diesel, petrol. Diagnosis to overhaul, no brand we won't open up.
about.crew_role3_body: Nav, comms, charging, batteries. Old systems, new systems, and everything that arrives wired wrong.
about.crew_role4_body: Haul-out, launch, transport. The people who make sure your boat moves safely on land.
about.seasons_subtitle: Four seasons, one rhythm — every boat has a place in it.
about.season1_body: Launches. Antifoul, polish, rig check, sea trial. The yard empties one boat at a time.
about.season2_body: Service & emergency repairs for the visiting fleet. Same-day diagnosis whenever the weather lets us.
about.season3_body: Haul-outs. Pressure-wash, cradle fit, walk-through report. The yard fills back up.
about.season4_body: Refits, rebuilds, custom construction. The slow work — the work the rest of the year doesn't allow.
about.values_title: Honest scope. Fair price. Boat back in the water.
about.values_body: Three rules we keep, even when they cost us a job. They are the reason owners come back, and the reason we still have a yard.
about.values_1_body: Written scope, fixed price, before the first tool comes out.
about.values_2_body: Surprises in the bilge are rare; surprises on the invoice are forbidden.
about.values_3_body: On every job we do in-house. Written. Boring. Real.
home.promise_title: A working yard, run by the people doing the work.
home.promise_p1: Triton Marine is a family-run boatyard on the harbor of Skala, Patmos. We haul, store, repair, and build — for the local fleet, for visiting yachts, and for owners who've trusted us with their boats for decades.
home.promise_p2: Twelve people, three trades, one yard. No call centre. The person who quotes the job is on the boat the next morning.
home.services_title: Four lines of work, one yard.
home.services_lead: From the everyday haul-out to a year-long custom build, every job runs on the same calendar — coordinated by the same crew, on the same harbor.
home.process_title: From the first call to keys-in-hand.
home.process_step1_body: Specs, photos, what's wrong, what you want. Email or phone — whatever is faster.
home.process_step2_body: Fixed price, scope of work, timeline. No work begins until you approve.
home.process_step3_body: Daily progress updates if the job runs more than a couple of days.
home.process_step4_body: Test in the water, walk-through, written warranty. Keys back.
home.proof_title: Twenty-eight years on the same harbor.
home.proof_body: Five hundred boats, twelve technicians, seven marina partners across the Dodecanese. A boatyard that's still here because every season, the boats come back.
home.proof_quote: "The boat went in for a winter refit. They sent weekly photos, called once with an honest scope change, and delivered on the day they promised. That's all I want from a yard."
services.intro_title: Four lines of work, one yard.
services.intro_p1: Haul-out and launch, parking and winter storage, repairs across mechanical/electrical/structural, and custom construction. Every line is run by the same crew, on the same harbor, on the same calendar.
services.intro_p2: Pick the service that fits your boat — or call the yard and we'll work out the right scope together.
services.list_lead: Each line below has its own page with process, FAQs, and pricing notes. Click through, or jump straight to the booking form at the bottom.
services.process_step1_body: Send us specs and photos, or bring the boat in. Diagnosis usually same-day.
services.process_step2_body: Fixed-price scope of work, in writing. We don't start until you sign off.
services.process_step3_body: Daily photo updates if the job runs more than two days. Owner visits welcome.
services.process_step4_body: Sea trial when relevant, written warranty on every job we do in-house.
service.benefit_1_body: Written quote before the first tool comes out.
service.benefit_2_body: The person who quotes the job is on the boat the next morning.
service.benefit_3_body: On every job we do in-house. Written certificate at handover.
service.benefit_4_body: Daily, when the job runs more than two days.
service.cta_body: Send us your boat's details and we'll come back within one business day with a quote and a date.
